title: Partial Route Visibility Across the OSPF Backbone After ABR Interface Change

ticket.summary: Since a maintenance window last night on abr-hq01, hosts on the R&D lab subnet (10.75.4.0/24, area 40) can reach some parts of the network but not others. Specifically, R&D can reach the HQ backbone (area 0) and area 40's other subnets fine, but cannot reach anything in the Sales area (area 20), and Sales users report R&D printers/servers as unreachable too. Other inter-area traffic (e.g. area 20 to area 0) is unaffected.

topology.description: abr-hq01 is an OSPF Area Border Router connecting area 0 (backbone), area 20 (Sales, via Gi0/1), and area 40 (R&D, via Gi0/2). All inter-area traffic between area 20 and area 40 must transit area 0 through abr-hq01, since OSPF requires all non-backbone areas to connect through area 0. During last night's maintenance, Gi0/2 was reconfigured as part of a VLAN cleanup and its OSPF area assignment was accidentally changed.

links.label: Gi0/2, intended Area 40 (misconfigured as Area 20 last night)

logs.content:
Jul 13 23:41:07 abr-hq01 %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console (VLAN cleanup - CHG0042117)
Jul 13 23:41:09 abr-hq01 %OSPF-5-ADJCHG: Process 1, Nbr 10.75.4.2 on GigabitEthernet0/2 from FULL to DOWN, Interface down or area changed
Jul 13 23:41:22 abr-hq01 %OSPF-5-ADJCHG: Process 1, Nbr 10.75.4.2 on GigabitEthernet0/2 from LOADING to FULL

Jul 14 08:15:03 rd-lab-sw01 %OSPF-5-ADJCHG: Process 1, Nbr 10.75.4.1 (abr-hq01) on Vlan40 from FULL to FULL
Jul 14 08:15:03 rd-lab-sw01 note: adjacency stable, but LSDB shows area 20 LSAs only, no area 0 summary LSAs for 10.75.2.0/24 received as expected for cross-area routing

cli_outputs.output:
Interface    PID   Area            IP Address/Mask    Cost  State Nbrs F/C
Gi0/0        1     0               10.75.0.1/30        1     P2P  1/1
Gi0/1        1     20              10.75.2.1/24         1     DR   3/3
Gi0/2        1     20              10.75.4.1/24         1     DR   2/2

Routing Process "ospf 1" with ID 10.75.0.1
 Area BACKBONE(0)
   Number of interfaces in this area is 1

   It is a stub area
 Area (40)
   Number of interfaces in this area is 0
   No interfaces currently active in area 40

monitoring.notes: The OSPF topology monitor shows abr-hq01 has reported zero active interfaces in area 40 since 23:41 last night, exactly matching the VLAN cleanup change window. Type-3 Summary LSAs for 10.75.4.0/24 that used to be injected into area 0 (and from there into area 20) stopped appearing at the same timestamp. Meanwhile area 20's summary LSA count increased by one, consistent with Gi0/2 having been merged into area 20 instead of area 40.

hints: R&D can reach area 0 fine but not Sales (area 20), and Sales can't reach R&D — but both can reach the backbone. That pattern suggests one area's routes aren't being summarized into area 0 correctly by the ABR.
Check which OSPF area each interface on abr-hq01 is actually assigned to versus which area it should be in.

Gi0/2 (facing the R&D subnet, 10.75.4.0/24) is configured for area 20 instead of area 40 following last night's VLAN cleanup — 'show ip ospf' confirms area 40 now has zero active interfaces on abr-hq01, so no Type-3 summary LSAs for R&D's real area are being generated.

solution.root_cause: Last night's VLAN cleanup on abr-hq01 accidentally reassigned interface Gi0/2 (facing the R&D lab subnet 10.75.4.0/24) from OSPF area 40 to area 20. Because area 40 now has no active interfaces on the ABR, no Type-3 summary LSAs for the R&D area are generated into the backbone, so area 20 (Sales) never learns a route to 10.75.4.0/24 and vice versa, even though both areas can still reach area 0 directly.
solution.fix: Reconfigure Gi0/2 on abr-hq01 back to OSPF area 40 so it correctly represents the R&D subnet as its own area, allowing abr-hq01 to generate proper Type-3 summary LSAs between area 40 and area 0 (and from there to area 20).
acceptable_answers: no active interfaces on the abr
